summary:
Chloroquine-related eye disease results from retinal toxicities due to binding to retinal pigment epithelial cells and inhibition of lysosomal function, independent of HIV mechanisms. None of the listed HIV-associated biological processes (protease, reverse transcriptase, integrase, or cellular entry) are clinically relevant to this adverse effect.  

(Note: This summary focuses only on critical clinical facts directly addressing the query.) 

End of Summary.
